perturbation.original_text: Except as expressly permitted herein, for a period of thr ee years from the effective da te (Non-Disclosure Period), Recipient shall maintain in confidence and not disclose Conf idential Information. Upon termination of this Agreement, Recipient’s right to use Conf idential Information, shall immediately terminate.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as expressly permitted herein, for a period of thr ee years from the effective da te (Non-Disclosure Period), Recipient shall maintain in confidence and not disclose Conf idential Information, unless both parties agree otherwise in writing. Upon termination of this Agreement, Recipient’s right to use Conf idential Information, shall immediately terminate.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds 'unless both parties agree otherwise in writing' to the clause regarding the Non-Disclosure Period. This contradicts section r which states 'Any modifications of or changes to this Ag reement shall be in writing and signed by both parties,' which means it needs to be in writing, but the previous modification is allowing it not to be. This could introduce uncertainty about whether modifications to the confidentiality period are allowed verbally or whether they must be formally written.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understa nding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, commitment and unde rstandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes to this Ag reement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'unless both parties agree otherwise in writing' to the Non-Disclosure Period clause. The contradicted text states 'Any modifications of or changes to this Agreement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.' The original text states that modifications to the non-disclosure agreement must be in writing, while the changed text allows for verbal agreements regarding the non-disclosure agreement, which contradicts the requirement for all modifications to be in writing as stated in the contradicted text. This creates a direct conflict and introduces uncertainty, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination, or upon demand by Discloser at any time , or upon expiration of this Agreement, Recipient shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Discloser’s option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information; provided, however, that Recipient may retain one copy of Discloser’s Confidential Information for archival purposes only.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination, or upon demand by Discloser at any time , or upon expiration of this Agreement, Recipient may choose to keep a copy and is not obligated to return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Discloser’s option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information; provided, however, that Recipient may retain one copy of Discloser’s Confidential Information for archival purposes only.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy' to 'may choose to keep a copy and is not obligated to return promptly to Discloser or destroy' we are contradicting what the original text is stating. Also, by removing the 'at Discloser's option' from the first part of the sentence we are creating more in-text contradiction. This directly goes against original agreement because it is not making it clear if confidential information has to be returned or not. This contradicts the provision's purpose of preventing information leaks. It could create uncertainty and lead to potential disputes over the control of confidential information after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon termination, or upon demand by Discloser at any time , or upon expiration of this Agreement, Recipient may choose to keep a copy and is not obligated to return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Discloser’s option, all tangible materials that disclose or embody Confidential Information; provided, however, that Recipient may retain one copy of Discloser’s Confidential Information for archival purposes only.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states the Recipient 'may choose to keep a copy and is not obligated to return promptly to Discloser or destroy', while the contradicted text implies the recipient might still need to destroy all tangible materials. The original text states the Recipient 'shall return promptly to Discloser or destroy, at Discloser’s option'. By changing the obligation from 'shall return or destroy' to 'may choose to keep' and removing 'at Discloser's option', the altered text introduces a direct contradiction because it's unclear if the recipient is required to return the confidential information or not. This clearly contradicts the original text, creating an inconsistency and uncertainty about the obligations, as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to accept the Confidential Information and to employ all reasonable efforts to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential, such efforts to be no less than the degree of care employed by Recipient to preserve and safeguard its own confidential information; prov ided however, that such efforts shall not be less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient does not have to accept the Confidential Information and may or may not employ reasonable efforts to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential, such efforts being or not being less than the degree of care employed by Recipient to preserve and safeguard its own confidential information; prov ided however, that such efforts shall not be less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by making the acceptance and safeguarding of confidential information optional. By changing 'Recipient agrees to accept the Confidential Information and to employ all reasonable efforts' to 'Recipient does not have to accept the Confidential Information and may or may not employ reasonable efforts' is contradictory and confusing. This contradicts the initial intention of the NDA and creates uncertainty about Recipient's obligations, undermining the entire purpose of the agreement. This section is directly contradicting itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient does not have to accept the Confidential Information and may or may not employ reasonable efforts to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential, such efforts being or not being less than the degree of care employed by Recipient to preserve and safeguard its own confidential information; prov ided however, that such efforts shall not be less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by making the acceptance and safeguarding of confidential information optional, whereas the original text mandates it. Specifically, the change from 'Recipient agrees to accept' to 'Recipient does not have to accept' creates a fundamental conflict. This directly undermines the NDA's purpose, as it creates uncertainty about the Recipient's obligations regarding confidentiality, contradicting the implied (and often explicit) expectations of a non-disclosure agreement. The changed text and the contradicted text are essentially the same, and because they explicitly allow for the Recipient to not accept confidential information, it is a clear, direct, and strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that Recipient is required by the Indiana Open Records Act, or any other law, to disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information, Recipient shall promptly notify Discloser, consult with Discloser re garding whether there are legitimate grounds to narrow or contest such disclosure, and only disclose that information that the University, in the opinion of legal counsel, is legally obligated to disclose.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that Recipient is required by the Indiana Open Records Act, or any other law, to disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information, Recipient shall not notify Discloser, consult with Discloser regarding whether there are legitimate grounds to narrow or contest such disclosure, and disclose that information that the Recipient chooses, without consulting legal counsel, to disclose.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Recipient shall promptly notify Discloser, consult with Discloser re garding whether there are legitimate grounds to narrow or contest such disclosure, and only disclose that information that the University, in the opinion of legal counsel, is legally obligated to disclose' to 'Recipient shall not notify Discloser, consult with Discloser regarding whether there are legitimate grounds to narrow or contest such disclosure, and disclose that information that the Recipient chooses, without consulting legal counsel, to disclose.' we are directly contradicting what the original text is stating. This removes the obligation for the Recipient to consult with the Discloser or legal counsel, potentially leading to unauthorized disclosure of confidential information and legal repercussions for the Discloser.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that Recipient is required by the Indiana Open Records Act, or any other law, to disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information, Recipient shall not notify Discloser, consult with Discloser regarding whether there are legitimate grounds to narrow or contest such disclosure, and disclose that information that the Recipient chooses, without consulting legal counsel, to disclose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The changed text states that the recipient "shall not notify Discloser" but the contradicted text is the same changed text. Thus, the contradicted text is directly contradicting itself, therefore making it a direct contradiction. Based on Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, without cause, effective imme diately upon written notice of termin ation; however, in the event this Agreement is terminated prior to expiration of the Non-Disc losure Period, its provisions shall survive and remain in effect for the remainder of the Non-Disclosure Period, with respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the effective date of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, without cause, effective imme diately upon written or verbal notice of termin ation; however, in the event this Agreement is terminated prior to expiration of the Non-Disc losure Period, its provisions shall not survive and remain in effect for the remainder of the Non-Disclosure Period, with respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the effective date of termination.
perturbation.explanation: By allowing verbal notice of termination, contradicts section r that states Any modifications of or changes to this Ag reement shall be in writing and signed by both parties. By removing the phrase 'its provisions shall survive and remain in effect for the remainder of the Non-Disclosure Period, with respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the effective date of termination' we are removing the protection on confidential information. This renders the NDA pointless, especially as section c describes that the recipient 'shall maintain in confidence and not disclose Conf idential Information', and if termination is the same as expiration of the non-disclosure period, by removing the line of protection during the period, we are creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as expressly permitted herein, for a period of thr ee years from the effective da te (Non-Disclosure Period), Recipient shall maintain in confidence and not disclose Conf idential Information. Upon termination of this Agreement, Recipient’s right to use Conf idential Information, shall immediately terminate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces two contradictions. First, it allows verbal notice of termination, which directly contradicts the requirement for written modifications elsewhere in the agreement (as described in the explanation). Second, it removes the survival clause for the non-disclosure period upon termination. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text which states that the recipient shall maintain confidentiality for three years (the Non-Disclosure Period). The change removes the protection on confidential information which makes the NDA pointless during the non-disclosure period as described in the explanation. This conflicts with the contradicted text.
